hero.subtitle: A visual archive for systemic nostalgia. Build modern classics on vintage hardware.
builder.label: System Interface
builder.title: The Builder
builder.desc: A visual IDE designed for the technical purist. Retruxel lets you place and configure game modules on a scene canvas, wire them to hardware events, and compile directly to authentic ROM files — no C required.
hardware.title: Compatible Hardware
hardware.desc: Native cross-compilation for the most iconic architectures of the golden era.
features.f1.title: Zero Setup Architecture
features.f1.desc: Retruxel ships with SDCC and cc65 toolchains for all supported targets. They are extracted automatically on first build — no manual compiler setup, no environment variables.
features.f2.title: Module System
features.f2.desc: Drag and drop pre-built modules — physics, input, entities, animation — onto the scene canvas and wire them to hardware events like VBlank and OnStart.
features.f3.title: One-Click ROM Export
features.f3.desc: Produce hardware-accurate ROM files instantly. Ready for flashcarts or professional distribution.
features.f4.title: Ready for Real Hardware
features.f4.desc: Retruxel outputs valid ROM files that run on original consoles and flashcarts. The generated C code targets real hardware constraints — VDP limits, scanline budgets, and Z80 or 6502 memory maps.
config.label: Architecture
config.title: Configure Once, Build Anywhere
config.desc: Every target in Retruxel ships with its own build profile — ROM size, region timing, emulator path, and launch behavior. Set it once per platform and Retruxel handles the rest at build time.
config.item1.title: Modules Compile Per Target
config.item1.desc: Each module has a dedicated code generator per platform. The same Physics or Input module produces correct C code for SMS, NES, or Game Gear automatically.
config.item2.title: Third-Party Plugin Support
config.item2.desc: The Retruxel SDK allows developers to ship custom modules and targets as plugins, extending the tool without modifying the core.
cta.title: Build the past.
cta.desc: Join the archive of developers breathing new life into the 8-bit aesthetic through systemic precision.
